title: IT Professional Elective Lec
subtitle: Module 1 & 2 Reviewer covering HTML Forms, JavaScript basics, Functions, and Control Structures.

questions.t: The HTTP _____ is used to send data to the server for further processing.
The _____ is used to request data from a specified resource or a resource from the server.
The _____ tag is used to specify pre-defined list options for input controls.
The _____ tag displays the output of the calculations performed.
The _____ attribute in the `<form>` element specifies how the data is sent to the server.
The _____ tag is used to draw a box around other form elements and group the related data.
The _____ tag defines a clickable button to control other elements or execute a functionality.
_____ is a section of the document that collects input from the user.
_____ is a software application used to write, edit, and save HTML code for creating web pages.
When sending data to a server using `method="post"`, the submitted user data is added directly into the body of the request, making it less visible to the user compared to the GET method.
If you use the standard `<input type="text">` element for a password field, the characters typed by the user will be clearly visible on the screen instead of being masked.
By wrapping form elements inside a `<fieldset>` tag, the browser will automatically draw a visual box boundary around them.
A developer is creating a survey form and wants to use the `<datalist>` element. The `<datalist>` tag is strictly used to display a read-only mathematical calculation result to the user.

A developer wants to create a gender selection field ("Male" or "Female"). If they use `<input type="radio">` tags but forget to give both elements the exact same `name` attribute, the user will be trapped into selecting both options simultaneously without being able to uncheck them.
You need to gather long paragraphs of user feedback. You should use an `<input type="text">` element because it natively provides a multi-line box that adjusts to long text contents.
If you set your form's configuration to `method="get"`, the data entered by the user will be appended directly to the URL string in the browser's address bar when submitted.

You want to create a web button that triggers a custom script without immediately submitting the form data to a server. You can explicitly configure the tag as `<button type="button">` to prevent the default submission behavior.
A teacher wants students to submit feedback that may include several sentences of free-form text. Which input element is most appropriate?
A web form is designed for a search engine's search bar, where the resulting query should be shareable as a bookmarkable link. Which HTTP method should the form use?

A developer wants users to pick a country from a long list of 195 countries. Comparing `<select>` with `<option>` elements versus `<datalist>` with `<input>`, which is generally the better user experience?
choices: Both are identical in function and UX

questions.t: A developer mistakenly omits the `name` attribute from all `<input>` fields in a form. When the form is submitted, the server receives no usable data. Why?
choices: The `name` attribute defines the key for sending data to the server
The `name` attribute only affects CSS styling, so the form should still work
Omitting `name` automatically converts the field to a `<button>`
The `id` attribute always replaces the need for `name` in form submissions
questions.a: The `name` attribute defines the key for sending data to the server

questions.t: A login form sends a user's password using the GET method, and the developer notices the password appears directly in the browser's address bar. What is the most appropriate fix?
choices: Switch to POST, as GET exposes sensitive data in the URL
Keep GET, but rename the input field to "secure_password"
Add a `<label>` tag to hide the password visually
questions.a: Switch to POST, as GET exposes sensitive data in the URL

questions.t: A registration form needs to let users select multiple hobbies where more than one option can be chosen at once. Which form element should be used?
A developer is building a job application form that includes a field for applicants to select their date of birth from a calendar widget. Which `<input>` type best fulfills this requirement?
A screen reader user reports that they cannot tell what a field is for given `<input type="text" name="username">`. What is missing?
choices: A `<button>` element, to let them submit faster
A `<label for="username">` element associated with the input's `id`
A `placeholder` attribute is sufficient and is identical to a `<label>` in function
questions.a: A `<label for="username">` element associated with the input's `id`

questions.t: How should `<fieldset>` and `<legend>` be used together to improve form structure?
choices: Each `<fieldset>` should visually/semantically group a related set of inputs, with a `<legend>` inside it acting as that group's caption
`<legend>` should wrap the entire form, while `<fieldset>` labels each input individually
`<fieldset>` and `<legend>` are interchangeable and serve the same purpose
Only one `<fieldset>` may exist per HTML document
questions.a: Each `<fieldset>` should visually/semantically group a related set of inputs, with a `<legend>` inside it acting as that group's caption

questions.t: Match each scenario to the correct HTML element.
Match each scenario to the correct form method or element.
JavaScript is an _____ language, which means scripts execute without preliminary compilation.
JavaScript is usually embedded directly into HTML pages using the _____ tag.
With the introduction of _____, JavaScript can now also be used for back-end web development.
A developer claims: "JavaScript requires you to purchase a license before you can use it in your projects."
JavaScript is classified as a compiled language because the browser must fully compile the script before any part of it can run.
A student argues that JavaScript can only be used for front-end web development and has no role in building back-end systems.
A web developer wants to check whether a user filled out all required fields in a registration form before the data ever reaches the server. Which JavaScript capability directly supports this?
choices: Writing dynamic text into an HTML page

questions.t: A game studio wants to build a casual game that runs directly inside a web browser without any plugin or download. Which of the following best describes why JavaScript is appropriate?
choices: JavaScript is a markup language designed for visual layouts
JavaScript can be used to build web-based games that run in the browser
JavaScript only runs on mobile devices with React Native installed
JavaScript requires NodeJS to run inside a browser
questions.a: JavaScript can be used to build web-based games that run in the browser

questions.t: Which of the following best describes what makes JavaScript a "scripting language"?
choices: It requires compilation before execution and has strict type enforcement
It is a heavyweight language used only for server-side processing
It is a lightweight programming language interpreted directly by the browser
It can only run after being converted into machine code by a separate compiler
questions.a: It is a lightweight programming language interpreted directly by the browser

questions.t: Match each scenario to the JavaScript capability it demonstrates.

a.A news site automatically updates the he: Put dynamic text into an HTML page

questions.t: JavaScript function logic is written between _____.
Arrow functions are not _____. They must be defined before they are used.
A function that is invoked automatically without being explicitly called is called an _____.
By default, every function that does not explicitly return a value returns _____.

A developer writes the following code and calls it before the function definition appears. This will throw a reference error because the function hasn't been defined yet.

```js
greet()
function greet() {
  console.log("Hello!")
}
```
A student claims that an external `.js` file can contain `<script>` tags inside it to organize code better.
Arrow functions eliminate the need to write the `function` keyword, the `return` keyword, and curly brackets when written in shorthand form.
A developer wants to output a value directly into the browser's developer console for debugging purposes. Which output method should they use?
A junior developer writes `let 2score = 100;`. Why is this invalid?
choices: Identifiers cannot start with a digit
The `let` keyword is reserved for strings only
Variable names cannot contain the letter "e"
questions.a: Identifiers cannot start with a digit

questions.t: A team of developers keeps introducing bugs by passing arguments to a many-parameter function in the wrong order. Which approach best solves this?
choices: Using the spread operator with an array
Using object destructuring to name parameters
Using an IIFE instead of a regular function
questions.a: Using object destructuring to name parameters

questions.t: Match each code pattern to the function type it represents.
The two main categories of control statements in JavaScript are _____ and _____.
questions.a: Conditional Control Statements, Looping/Iterative Control Statements

questions.t: In a `switch` case, the _____ statement is used to terminate a sequence and prevent fall-through to the next case.
A `do...while` loop checks its condition _____ executing the block of code, making it an exit control loop.
A developer writes a `switch` statement but forgets to include any `break` statements between cases. The program will still only execute the matched case and stop automatically.
A `do...while` loop guarantees that the code block runs at least once, even if the condition is false from the start.
A `while` loop is best used when you already know exactly how many times the loop needs to run before it starts.
A student is building a grade classifier that checks if a score is "Excellent", "Passing", or "Failing" based on ranges. Which control structure is most appropriate?
An e-commerce system needs to apply discount rates based on customer type: "senior" gets 12%, "regular" gets 10%, and anything else gets 0%. Which structure directly demonstrates this pattern?
A developer needs a loop that keeps prompting the user until the correct PIN is entered, but must always show the prompt at least once. Which loop fits this requirement?
Match each scenario to the correct control structure.
